title: cornus stolonifera f. flaviramea
intro: Cornus stolonifera f. flaviramea, also known as yellow-flowering dogwood, is a shrubby dogwood species native to North America, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and red berries. Traditionally, the plant's bark and roots have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Cornus stolonifera f. flaviramea include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal preparations.
